metadata.title: GardenOS — Smart garden & robot mowing
metadata.description: mygardenos.com: digitized gardens and robot mowing subscriptions.

hero.title: It's the AI era — why is mowing still so expensive and such a hassle?
hero.subtitle: Hire someone: expensive, and you still have to wait. DIY: tiring, and it eats your weekend. Buy a robot: expensive, and it might not fit your garden.
hero.thirdLine: GardenOS is running real-garden tests in Auckland. We test for you — if it's good, we buy together; if not, we keep looking. Free to join.
hero.cta: Reserve My Spot for the Live Test →

item1.label: Prototype status
item1.value: Shenzhen → on the way by sea, ETA Auckland ~40 days

pitfalls.title: We take the risks for you
pitfalls.body: There are plenty of robot mowers out there — and some look cheap online. But Auckland gardens have slopes, trees, narrow passages, and complex boundaries. Buying a robot isn't hard. The hard part is whether it can work reliably in real Auckland gardens. We'll buy them, test them, and you watch. If it's good enough, we buy together. If not, we keep looking. No edits. No polishing. No hiding failures. If it gets stuck, you'll see it. If it can't handle boundaries, you'll see it. If battery life falls short, you'll see it.

scenarios.title: Six real scenarios that match Auckland's most common garden challenges
scenarios.subtitle: Test location: a real home garden in Botany, Auckland (two different terrains) | Format: live stream, viewers ask questions in real time

s1.title: Slope test
s1.body: Typical Auckland sloped lawns — can it climb and descend without slipping?

s2.title: Obstacle test
s2.body: Garden beds, roots, stones, kids' toys — real obstacles and avoidance behavior

s3.title: Narrow passage test
s3.body: Tight corridors between fences or walls — can it pass through cleanly?

s4.title: Complex boundaries
s4.body: Irregular garden shapes — boundary accuracy and edge cutting quality

s5.title: Real battery life
s5.body: Does real coverage match the manufacturer's claims?

s6.title: Noise test
s6.body: Will neighbors tolerate it? We'll measure dB live.

scenarios.ending: After the live test, viewers vote: is it good enough? If yes, we organize a group buy. If no, we move on to the next brand.

priorityPlan.title: Test participation plan: your garden decides what we test
priorityPlan.body: Want GardenOS to test closer to your real garden? Complete three steps to help shape the next live test:

p1.title: Reserve the live stream
p1.body: We'll notify you first before the live stream starts.

p2.title: Share your garden details
p2.body: Tell us about area, slope, trees, garden beds, narrow passages, and complex boundaries.

p3.title: Co-design the next test
p3.body: Want to see wet grass, tall grass, slopes, obstacle avoidance, noise, or a specific brand? Your feedback will shape what we test next. You can also upload photos, videos, floor plans, or 3D scan files.

priorityPlan.cta: Submit my garden details — help shape the next test →
priorityPlan.note: This info isn't for collecting privacy. It's to help us design test scenarios that better match real home gardens.
priorityPlan.aside: Note: uploads live in the dashboard. If you're not signed in, you'll be prompted first.

alliance.title: Homeowner alliance logic: people helping people
alliance.body:
GardenOS isn't a platform that sells robots.
We're a real-test alliance built with Auckland homeowners.
Which mower truly fits Auckland gardens?
Not the manufacturers — and not us — but real-world tests decide.

a1.title: Free to participate
a1.body: Live-stream RSVPs, test suggestions, and feedback are free.

a2.title: Your garden shapes the tests
a2.body: Slopes, narrow passages, roots, complex boundaries? What you share influences what we test next.

a3.title: Recommend brands to test
a3.body: Heard of a robot mower worth trying? Tell us — popular recommendations enter the shortlist for future tests.

a4.title: Find the right Auckland setup together
a4.body: Every piece of feedback and every garden scenario helps us get closer to a truly local-fit robot mowing solution.

alliance.cta: Reserve the live stream — help shape the next test →

about.metaDescription: The GardenOS team, the mowing problems we're solving, real-garden testing and live streams, and what we promise homeowners.
about.title: Who We Are & Why We Built This
about.origin: We're a small team in Auckland. Mowing should be more predictable and more transparent. GardenOS starts with real-garden testing — using live streams and records to show what actually works.

mission.title: What we're doing
mission.body:
· 3D-scan your garden to keep a record
· Test robots repeatedly in real gardens
· Use live streams so you can see real results

We don't sell machines first — we test the problem first.
We'll try robot after robot to see what truly fits Auckland gardens.

vision.title: Our goal
vision.body:
For homes with gardens, mowing shouldn't be a constant headache.
No endless rescheduling, no weekends lost to the mower,
and no fear of buying the wrong machine.

problems.title: Three things we want to fix

p1.title: Mowing is expensive — and every handover starts from zero
p1.body: Every time someone new shows up, you explain the garden again. Nothing is on record — it's all memory. Timing is shaky too, so plans keep shifting.

p2.title: Human mowing is inconsistent — hard to rely on long term
p2.body: Peak season is hard to book; off-season feels random. After a crew change, quality swings. It's tough to keep one steady approach.

p3.title: You pay — but you can't see what really happened
p3.body: Did they mow today? How well? Often it's guesswork. No data, no paper trail.

v1.title: Your garden data belongs to you
v1.body: The robot works in your garden. Safety first, and you have full control of all data.

v2.title: No ad-style demos — only real tests
v2.body: No polished edits, no hiding the rough edges. If it gets stuck, you'll see it. If it's not good enough, you'll see that too.

v3.title: Your feedback sets our next step
v3.body: We're still early. Every test direction shifts based on what homeowners tell us.

cta.title: Want to try a robot in your garden?
cta.body: We're recruiting the first batch of test gardens in Auckland. The first 200 people can join real tests early and receive a 3D scan record of their garden.

participate.metaDescription: Three ways to join GardenOS: register free, book a garden scan, join a live mowing demo — earlier is cheaper.
participate.title: Three ways to join — the earlier, the cheaper
participate.lead: Homeowners, property operators, and technical partners can all contribute—the paths below turn your signal into roadmap decisions.

step1.title: Register free, lock in your price
step1.body: Join the waitlist for price-drop alerts, events, and your early-bird rate.

step2.title: Book your free garden scan
step2.body: First 200 members can book a professional 3D scan—slots and timing confirmed by email.

step3.title: Join a live mowing demo
step3.body: Watch the robot mow in public sessions—we'll send RSVP and watch links when dates are set.

participate.ctaBody: RSVP for the next public test, or create a membership to hold launch perks and notifications.

live.metaDescription: Watch the robot mow live, catch replays, and read what's new for GardenOS public tests.
live.title: Watch the robot mow — live
live.lead: We run open prototype tests on a rolling schedule. Register once, and we'll notify you the moment a date is confirmed.
live.windowBody: No date locked yet. When the next session is confirmed, we'll share the RSVP link, what we're testing, and how to watch.
live.windowNote: If you register, you'll get notified when it's official. If you can't make it live, we'll post the recap and replay link here.
live.channelsTitle: How to watch and download assets

ch1.title: Live stream
ch1.body: Public sessions include multi-camera streams and captions—join five minutes early for the safety briefing.

ch2.title: Watch replay
ch2.body: Edited replays and written recaps will be posted within 48 hours, including what was tested and any known limitations.

ch3.title: What's new
ch3.body: After each session, we share what changed, what worked, and what we're still figuring out.

live.bannerBody: Create a membership and enable email alerts to get RSVP and watch links the moment a schedule drops.

liveTest.metaDescription: Internal YouTube live integration testing page with a configurable stream URL.
liveTest.title: YouTube Live Integration Test
liveTest.lead: Use this page to verify YouTube live stream embedding before publishing public events.
liveTest.configBody: You can edit the URL below for instant switching during tests, or update public/config/live-stream.json as the default source.
liveTest.envEmpty: No stream URL is active yet.
liveTest.inputHint: Click Apply to switch immediately without restarting the service.
liveTest.previewHint: Supported examples: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VIDEO_ID, https://youtu.be/VIDEO_ID, or https://www.youtube.com/embed/VIDEO_ID.
liveTest.previewUnavailable: Preview unavailable because the stream URL is not configured.
liveTest.fallbackBody: After setting the URL, reload this page to verify playback and sharing links.
